Potato.tagline: The world's most consumed vegetable — humble, filling, and infinitely versatile.
Potato.about: Potatoes originated in the Andes mountains of South America and were introduced to Europe by Spanish explorers in the 16th century. Today they are the world's fourth-largest food crop. India is among the top global producers, with UP, West Bengal, and Bihar leading production.
micronutrients: Potassium — 421mg (12% DV). More than a banana. Supports heart and muscle function.
Vitamin C — 19.7mg. Concentrated under the skin — keep skin on when possible.
Vitamin B6 — supports brain health and mood regulation.
benefits: Energy — complex carbohydrates provide sustained, slow-release energy.
Heart health — potassium and fiber support healthy blood pressure.
Gut health — resistant starch (especially in cooked-and-cooled potatoes) feeds beneficial bacteria.
Satiety — one of the most filling foods per calorie in studies.
cautions: High glycemic index when baked or mashed — diabetics should prefer boiled or cooled potatoes.
Avoid green-tinted potatoes — they contain solanine which is toxic in large amounts.
Deep-frying dramatically increases calorie and fat content.
Potato.india_note: Available year-round across India via cold storage. Best fresh crop harvested Jan–Mar in UP, West Bengal, and Bihar.
Potato.global_note: China is the world's largest producer. Available year-round globally via cold storage.
tips: Store in a cool, dark, well-ventilated place — never in the refrigerator (converts starch to sugar).
Keep away from onions — they release gases that spoil each other faster.
Choose firm potatoes with no sprouts, green tinge, or soft spots.
Cooling cooked potatoes increases resistant starch — great for gut health.
culinary_tips: Boil with skin on to retain maximum nutrients — peel after if desired.
For crispier roast potatoes: parboil, roughen edges, and roast in hot oil.
Add cold boiled potatoes to salads — the resistant starch is a gut prebiotic.
Make aloo paratha by stuffing spiced mashed potato in whole wheat dough.
avoid_with: Store away from onions and bananas (ethylene gas speeds sprouting)
growing_tips: Plant seed potatoes (not regular potatoes) for disease-free crop.
Best grown Oct–Jan in Indian plains for a March harvest.
Earth up (mound soil around stems) as plants grow to increase yield.
Can be grown in large bags or containers — ideal for urban gardens.
fun_facts: Potatoes were the first vegetable grown in space (on the Space Shuttle Columbia in 1995).
The average Indian consumes over 20kg of potatoes per year.
There are over 4,000 varieties of potato grown worldwide.

Tomato.tagline: Technically a fruit, practically a vegetable — the backbone of global cuisines.
Tomato.about: Tomatoes originated in South America and were introduced to Europe by Spanish explorers in the 16th century. Today they are one of the world's most consumed vegetables. India is the third-largest global producer with Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, and Maharashtra leading output.
micronutrients: Lycopene — powerful carotenoid antioxidant. Concentration doubles when cooked.
Vitamin C — 14mg (23% DV). Supports immunity and collagen production.
benefits: Cancer protection — lycopene linked to reduced prostate and breast cancer risk.
Heart health — folate and potassium support cardiovascular function.
Skin health — lycopene and Vitamin C protect against UV-induced damage.
Eye health — lutein and zeaxanthin reduce macular degeneration risk.
cautions: Nightshade family — may aggravate joint inflammation in sensitive individuals.
High acidity can worsen acid reflux and GERD.
Avoid unripe green tomatoes in large amounts — contain solanine.
Tomato.india_note: Available year-round across India. Peak quality Dec–Feb. Karnataka, AP, and Maharashtra are top producers.
Tomato.global_note: China is the world's largest producer. Greenhouse production ensures year-round global availability.
tips: Choose firm, deeply coloured tomatoes that are heavy for their size.
Smell the stem end — ripe tomatoes have a strong earthy fragrance.
Never refrigerate tomatoes — cold destroys their flavour and texture.
Store stem-side down at room temperature for up to 5 days.
culinary_tips: Roast at high heat to concentrate flavour for sauces and soups.
Score the base and blanch 30 sec for easy peeling.
Salt cut tomatoes 15 min before use to remove excess moisture.
Sun-dry in the oven at low heat to intensify sweetness.
growing_tips: Needs full sun (6+ hrs) and very consistent watering.
Stake or cage plants at 30cm height to support heavy fruit.
Pinch off suckers (side shoots) to direct energy into fruit.
Inconsistent watering causes blossom end rot — keep soil moisture even.
fun_facts: Tomatoes are botanically fruits — they are the seed-bearing part of the plant.
The word 'tomato' comes from the Aztec word 'tomatl'.
There are over 10,000 tomato varieties grown worldwide.

Carrot.tagline: Crunchy, sweet, and bursting with beta-carotene — the original superfood.
Carrot.about: Carrots were originally purple and yellow — the familiar orange variety was selectively developed in the Netherlands in the 17th century. India is among the top global producers. The winter carrot crop in Punjab and Haryana is considered among the sweetest in the world.
micronutrients: Beta-carotene — 8285µg. Converts to Vitamin A; absorption increases significantly when cooked with fat.
Vitamin K1 — essential for blood clotting and bone health.
benefits: Eye health — beta-carotene converts to Vitamin A, preventing night blindness.
Cancer protection — carotenoid antioxidants linked to reduced cancer risk.
Heart health — fiber and potassium support blood pressure and cholesterol.
Gut health — high fiber content feeds beneficial bacteria.
cautions: Eating very large amounts can cause carotenemia — harmless yellowing of the skin.
Higher glycemic index when cooked — diabetics may prefer raw carrots.
Carrot.india_note: Best quality in winter months. Punjab, Haryana, and UP are top producers. Available year-round in metro markets via cold chain.
Carrot.global_note: China is the world's largest producer. Available year-round globally.
tips: Choose firm, smooth carrots with bright colour and no cracks or soft spots.
Fresh tops (if attached) should be green and perky — not wilted.
Remove greens before storing — they draw moisture from the root.
Refrigerate in a sealed bag for up to 3 weeks.
culinary_tips: Cook lightly to preserve nutrients — steaming is better than boiling.
Pair with fat (butter, oil) to maximise beta-carotene absorption.
Grate raw into salads, coleslaws, and raitas for crunch and colour.
Roast with honey and cumin for a simple Indian-inspired side.
growing_tips: Grow in loose, deep, stone-free soil — rocks cause forked, misshapen roots.
Sow seeds directly — carrots do not transplant well.
Thin seedlings to 5cm apart after germination for proper root development.
Best sown Oct–Feb in Indian plains for a winter-spring harvest.
fun_facts: Original wild carrots were purple or yellow — not orange.
The largest carrot ever grown weighed over 10kg.

Spinach.tagline: One of the most nutrient-dense leafy greens on the planet.
Spinach.about: Spinach was first cultivated in ancient Persia and reached Europe via Arab trade routes. It is a staple in Indian cuisine as palak and is used worldwide in salads, smoothies, and cooked dishes. China produces over 90% of global spinach supply.
micronutrients: Vitamin K — 483µg per 100g (403% DV). Critical for blood clotting and bone mineralisation.
Iron — 2.7mg. Non-heme iron — pair with Vitamin C for best absorption.
Folate — 194µg. Essential during pregnancy for neural tube development.
benefits: Bone health — exceptional Vitamin K content supports bone mineralisation.
Blood health — iron and folate support healthy red blood cell production.
Eye health — lutein and zeaxanthin protect against cataracts and macular degeneration.
Blood pressure — dietary nitrates help relax blood vessels and lower pressure.
cautions: High in oxalates — people prone to kidney stones should limit consumption.
Very high Vitamin K may interfere with blood thinners (warfarin) — consult a doctor.
Cooking reduces oxalate content and makes iron more bioavailable.
Spinach.india_note: Available year-round; best quality in winter. Widely used across all Indian cuisines as palak.
Spinach.global_note: China produces over 90% of world spinach. Prefers cool weather globally.
tips: Choose dark green leaves with no yellowing, wilting, or sliminess.
Baby spinach is milder and more tender than mature spinach.
Refrigerate unwashed in a sealed bag for up to 5 days.
Wash just before use — excess moisture speeds spoilage.
culinary_tips: Wilt in a hot pan with garlic and oil in under 2 minutes.
Add raw to smoothies — flavour is masked by banana or mango.
Blanch and squeeze dry before adding to curries to prevent wateriness.
Pair with paneer, lentils, or eggs for a protein-complete meal.
avoid_with: Milk at the same time (Ayurvedic caution)
growing_tips: Cool-season crop — sow Oct–Feb across most Indian plains.
Grows well in containers — needs only 15cm depth.
Bolts (flowers) quickly in heat — harvest before this happens.
Succession sow every 2 weeks for a continuous harvest.
fun_facts: Spinach contains more protein per calorie than most vegetables.
The iron in spinach is non-heme iron — absorbed best with Vitamin C.
NASA has studied spinach as a candidate crop for growing in space.

Onion.tagline: The humble foundation of every great dish — raw, cooked, or caramelised.
Onion.about: Onions are among the oldest cultivated vegetables, grown for over 5,000 years. India is the world's second-largest producer and the leading exporter. Onions are central to Indian cooking and hold cultural significance — Indian onion prices are even a political issue.
micronutrients: Quercetin — powerful flavonoid antioxidant, highest in outer layers and red onions.
Allicin precursors — released when cut, providing antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects.
Fructooligosaccharides — prebiotic fiber that feeds beneficial gut bacteria.
benefits: Heart health — quercetin reduces LDL oxidation and lowers blood pressure.
Antibacterial — allicin compounds inhibit E. coli, Salmonella, and Staphylococcus.
Blood sugar control — chromium and quercetin improve insulin sensitivity.
Gut health — prebiotic fiber feeds beneficial gut bacteria.
cautions: Can cause bad breath and body odour due to sulfur compounds.
May cause heartburn in people with acid reflux.
Toxic to dogs and cats — do not feed onions to pets.
Onion.india_note: Available year-round. Maharashtra (Nashik) is India's onion capital. Prices spike in monsoon months due to limited fresh supply.
Onion.global_note: China and India are the top two producers globally. Available year-round worldwide.
tips: Choose firm onions with dry, papery skin and no soft spots or sprouting.
Store in a cool, dry, well-ventilated place — not in the fridge.
Keep away from potatoes — they absorb moisture and gas from each other.
Refrigerate cut onions in an airtight container for up to 5 days.
culinary_tips: Chill onions in the fridge 30 min before cutting to reduce eye irritation.
Caramelise slowly over low heat 30–40 min for natural sweetness.
Raw thin slices with lemon and salt make a classic Indian salad.
Fry until golden-brown for birista — the crispy topping for biryanis.
avoid_with: Pets — toxic to dogs and cats
growing_tips: Grow from sets (small bulbs) for faster, easier results than seed.
Best sown Oct–Nov in Indian plains for a Feb–Mar harvest.
Needs full sun and well-drained soil — waterlogging causes rot.
Stop watering when tops fall over — this signals the bulb is mature.
fun_facts: Onions make you cry because cutting releases syn-propanethial-S-oxide, which reacts with eye moisture to form sulfuric acid.
Ancient Egyptians worshipped onions and placed them in pharaoh tombs.
India is the world's largest onion exporter.

Sweet Potato.tagline: Versatile root packed with natural sweetness and vibrant color.
Sweet Potato.about: Sweet potatoes are a staple in many cuisines, especially in India where they're boiled, roasted, or fried. India ranks among top producers, with major cultivation in Uttar Pradesh and Bihar.
micronutrients: Beta-carotene — converts to vitamin A, highest in orange varieties.
Anthocyanins — antioxidants in purple types, fight inflammation.
Fiber — supports digestion and blood sugar stability.
benefits: Immune boost — vitamin A and C strengthen defenses.
Blood sugar control — low glycemic index despite sweetness.
cautions: High carb content — portion control for diabetics.
Raw consumption can cause digestive upset due to oxalates.
Avoid green spots — indicate solanine toxin.
Sweet Potato.india_note: Year-round availability, peaks post-monsoon. Affordable staple in rural diets.
Sweet Potato.global_note: China leads production; widely exported year-round.
tips: Pick firm tubers with smooth, unblemished skin.
Store in cool, dark place — avoid fridge to prevent starch conversion.
Do not wash until use; lasts 1-2 months.
culinary_tips: Roast at 200°C for 45 min with oil and spices.
Boil and mash with ghee for Indian-style sabzi.
Make halwa by grating and cooking with milk.
Fry thin chips for healthy snack.
growing_tips: Plant slips in loose soil during monsoon.
Space 30cm apart in mounds to prevent rot.
fun_facts: Not a potato — belongs to morning glory family.
Orange color from beta-carotene, selectively bred in US.
NASA studied them for space farming.

Garlic.tagline: Nature's antibiotic — punches up every savory dish.
Garlic.about: Garlic is essential in Indian tadka and global cuisines. India is second-largest producer after China.
benefits: Heart health — lowers cholesterol and blood pressure.
cautions: Can thin blood — caution with anticoagulants.
Toxic to pets in large amounts.
Garlic.india_note: Indore (MP) produces premium garlic. Year-round but winter best.
tips: Buy firm bulbs with intact paper skin.
Store in cool, dark place — lasts months.
Sprouted still good — just remove sprout.
culinary_tips: Crush and rest 10 min before cooking to activate allicin.
Roast whole bulbs for sweet spread.
Make garlic pickle with oil and chili.
Single clove garlic (lashun) for milder flavor.
growing_tips: Plant cloves pointed end up in Oct-Nov.
fun_facts: Garlic was used in WWII as penicillin substitute.
Egyptians fed it to pyramid builders.
Vampire repellent myth from real antimicrobial properties.

Beetroot.tagline: Earthy sweetness with stunning ruby color.
Beetroot.about: Beets stain everything red but pack serious nutrition. Popular in Indian salads and borscht worldwide.
benefits: Liver detox — betalains support phase II detox.
cautions: High oxalates — caution for kidney stones.
May lower blood pressure too much.
Beetroot.india_note: Winter crop in Punjab, Maharashtra. Boiled with chaat masala classic.
tips: Choose heavy beets with greens attached.
Store in fridge crisper up to 3 months.
Cook with skin on, rub off after.
culinary_tips: Grate raw for salads with lemon.
growing_tips: Harvest at golf ball size for tenderness.
fun_facts: Napoleon used beet sugar against British blockade.

Radish.tagline: Spicy crunch that grows in weeks — salad's best friend.
Radish.about: Radishes are the fastest vegetable to grow. Mula is popular in Indian cooking, especially Bengali cuisine.
cautions: Goitrogenic raw — limit if thyroid issues.
Radish.india_note: Winter crop everywhere. White mooli for sabzi, red for salads.
tips: Store in fridge up to 1 month.
culinary_tips: Grate with green chili for raita.
growing_tips: Sow every 2 weeks for continuous harvest.

Turnip.tagline: Underrated root with peppery bite and tender greens.
Turnip.about: Turnips store well and both roots and greens are edible. Shalgam in North Indian cooking.
cautions: Goitrogenic raw — cook if thyroid issues.
Turnip.india_note: Shalgam popular in Punjab winter. Pickle and curry.
tips: Store in fridge 2 weeks or root cellar months.
culinary_tips: Boil young turnips whole with skin.
Shalgam ka achar famous Punjabi pickle.

Rutabaga.tagline: Sweeter, larger cousin of turnip — winter storage star.
Rutabaga.about: Rutabaga (swede) stores for months. Less common in India but nutritious.
Rutabaga.india_note: Rare fresh, imported occasionally. Substitute large turnip.

Celeriac.tagline: Celery's ugly root cousin with nutty flavor.
Celeriac.about: Celeriac (celery root) shines in remoulade. Rare in India.

Cassava.about: Cassava feeds 800M people. Major crop in Kerala, Tamil Nadu.
benefits: Gut health — resistant starch feeds good bacteria.
Cassava.india_note: Kerala tapioca staple. Chips, puttu, kappa biriyani.

Ginger.tagline: Fiery rhizome that warms body and soul.
Ginger.about: India produces 35% of world's ginger. Adrak essential in every Indian kitchen.
Ginger.india_note: Kerala #1 producer. Fresh year-round, dry ginger from Cochin.
growing_tips: Plant rhizomes with buds up June.
fun_facts: Worth more than gold in Middle Ages.

Turmeric.tagline: Golden spice with superfood healing power.
Turmeric.about: India produces 80% world turmeric. Haldi in every Indian home.
Turmeric.india_note: Erode (TN) turmeric capital. Lakadong (Meghalaya) highest curcumin.

Daikon.tagline: Giant mild radish for pickling and purity.
Daikon.about: Mooli/daikon popular in Japan, Korea, India. Excellent digestive.
benefits: Super digestive — enzymes break down starch, protein.
Daikon.india_note: White mooli = daikon. Grated raita, steamed sabzi.

Parsnip.tagline: Sweet winter carrot cousin — frost makes magic.
Parsnip.about: Parsnips improve with frost. Rare in India but excellent roasted.

Kale.tagline: Nutrient powerhouse leafy green — king of superfoods.
Kale.about: Kale exploded in popularity for nutrition. Gaining traction in urban Indian gardens.
cautions: Goitrogenic raw — rotate with thyroid issues.
Kale.india_note: Cool hill stations. Kale chips trendy urban.

Cabbage.tagline: Crisp layers of versatility — from salad to sauerkraut.
Cabbage.taste: Mild, sweet when cooked, peppery raw
Cabbage.about: India 2nd largest producer. Bandh gobi in every kitchen.
Cabbage.india_note: Maharashtra, UP major. Sabzi, paratha, Manchurian.
fun_facts: Sauerkraut fed to sailors vs. scurvy.

Lettuce.tagline: Crisp hydration with endless salad variety.
Lettuce.about: Salad essential. Hydroponic boom in India metros.
Lettuce.india_note: Hydroponics Pune, Bangalore. Salad bars boom.

Swiss Chard.tagline: Colorful stems, mild spinach taste — ornamental edible.
Swiss Chard.about: Stems add crunch/color. Rare India but grows well.

Mustard Greens.tagline: Spicy heat that cuts through richness.
Mustard Greens.taste: Sharp mustard bite, less bitter cooked
Mustard Greens.about: Sarson ka saag Punjabi winter staple with makki ki roti.
Mustard Greens.india_note: Punjab sarson saag season. Rai for seeds.

Bok Choy.tagline: Baby Chinese cabbage — tender stems, mild flavor.
Bok Choy.about: Pak choi boom in India with Chinese food popularity. Grows fast.

Collard Greens.tagline: Southern soul food — tough leaves turn silky slow-cooked.
Collard Greens.taste: Mild cabbage, less bitter than kale
Collard Greens.about: Collards gaining popularity in India for nutrition. Tough leaves need cooking.
Collard Greens.india_note: Rare but grows like sarson. Slow-cook tadka.

Arugula.tagline: Peppery salad rocket — gourmet pepper without calories.
Arugula.taste: Nutty pepper, mustard bite intensifies with age
Arugula.about: Rocket salad trendy in India urban dining. Grows anywhere.
Arugula.india_note: Roquette in fancy salads. Terrace garden star.

Romaine.tagline: Crisp Caesar king — sturdy leaves hold dressing.
Romaine.about: Cos/romaine essential Caesar. Reliable India grower.

Broccoli.tagline: Mini trees packed with cancer-fighting sulforaphane.
Broccoli.taste: Mild cabbage, grassy raw, sweet roasted
Broccoli.about: India production booming. Hari phoolgobhi nutritious.

Cauliflower.tagline: Versatile blank canvas — rice, mash, roast, fry.
Cauliflower.about: India #2 producer after China. Phoolgobhi everyday sabzi.
Cauliflower.india_note: Punjab, UP winter king. Aloo gobi, paratha, pakora.

Brussels Sprouts.tagline: Mini cabbages that taste better after frost.
Brussels Sprouts.about: Cold weather crop. Rare India but hill stations.

Kohlrabi.tagline: Alien spaceship veggie — crunchy cabbage turnip.
Kohlrabi.about: German turnip. Growing popularity India for crunch.

Cabbage Red.latin: Brassica oleracea var. capitata f. rubra
Cabbage Red.taste: Peppier than green, turns blue cooking
Cabbage Red.about: Red cabbage slaw favorite. India salads.

Bell Pepper.tagline: Sweet rainbow crunch — vitamin C bombs.
Bell Pepper.about: Shimla mirch India favorite. Color = ripeness stage.

Chili Pepper.tagline: Heat engine — capsaicin painkiller and metabolism booster.
Chili Pepper.taste: Burning heat varies wildly by variety
Chili Pepper.about: India #1 chili producer. Mirch defines spice level everywhere.

Cucumber.about: India origin! Kakdi summer staple raita, salad.

Zucchini.tagline: Prolific summer squash — noodles, fries, bread.
Zucchini.about: Italian zucchini boom India urban cooking.
Zucchini.india_note: Italian veg trend. Zucchini sabzi, grilled.

Eggplant.india_note: Every state dish. Bharta, curry, fry.

Okra.tagline: Slippery pods with mucilage magic — bhindi rules.
Okra.about: Bhindi India's favorite. Lady's finger debated origin.
Okra.india_note: Everywhere summer. Bhindi masala, fry, sabzi.

Pumpkin.tagline: Halloween icon, sweet creamy flesh for pies and curries.
Pumpkin.about: Kaddu India Diwali sweet. Pumpkins = specific varieties.
Pumpkin.india_note: Kaddu sabzi, halwa Diwali. Sugar baby mini.

Butternut Squash.tagline: Smooth sweet pear-shaped — roast, puree perfection.

Acorn Squash.tagline: Sweet nutty mini acorns — perfect stuffed boats.
Acorn Squash.about: Rare India. Small size apartment friendly.

Parsley.tagline: Nutrient bomb garnish — more than plate decoration.

Coriander.tagline: Love it/hate it — genetic! Dhania transforms dishes.
Coriander.about: Dhania patta India kitchen staple. Genetic taste divide.

Cilantro.tagline: Fresh citrusy burst — dhania patta genetic love/hate.
Cilantro.about: Dhania India EVERY dish. Same plant coriander.
Cilantro.india_note: Dhania patta market staple. Chatni, garnish.

Mint.tagline: Cool refreshing — pudina cools body and breath.

Rosemary.tagline: Piney memory herb — roast lamb essential.
Rosemary.about: Rosemary rare India but growing grilling trend.

Thyme.tagline: Tiny leaves mighty medicine — thyme kills germs.

Basil.tagline: Sweet spicy holy tulsi cousin — pesto perfection.
Basil.about: Tulsi India sacred. Italian basil different.

Lettuce Iceberg.tagline: Crunchy iceberg cool core — durable salad base.
Lettuce Iceberg.about: Iceberg India burgers, salads. Crispest lettuce.
